([Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin"): *Dioecesis Gandavensis*) is a [Latin Church](/wiki/Latin_Church "Latin Church") ecclesiastical territory or [diocese](/wiki/Diocese "Diocese") of the [Catholic Church in Belgium](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Belgium "Catholic Church in Belgium"). It is a [suffragan](/wiki/Suffragan "Suffragan") in the [ecclesiastical province](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_province "Ecclesiastical province") of the [metropolitan](/wiki/Metropolis_(religious_jurisdiction) "Metropolis (religious jurisdiction)") [Archdiocese of Mechelen-Brussels](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Mechelen-Brussels "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Mechelen-Brussels"). The patron of the diocese is Saint [Bavo of Ghent](/wiki/Bavo_of_Ghent "Bavo of Ghent").

The **Diocese of Ghent** ([Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin"): *Dioecesis Gandavensis*) is a [Latin Church](/wiki/Latin_Church "Latin Church") ecclesiastical territory or [diocese](/wiki/Diocese "Diocese") of the [Catholic Church in Belgium](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_Belgium "Catholic Church in Belgium"). It is a [suffragan](/wiki/Suffragan "Suffragan") in the [ecclesiastical province](/wiki/Ecclesiastical_province "Ecclesiastical province") of the [metropolitan](/wiki/Metropolis_(religious_jurisdiction) "Metropolis (religious jurisdiction)") [Archdiocese of Mechelen-Brussels](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Mechelen-Brussels "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Mechelen-Brussels"). The patron of the diocese is Saint [Bavo of Ghent](/wiki/Bavo_of_Ghent "Bavo of Ghent").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The diocese was erected in 1559 by papal bull *Super universas* to become independent of the Diocese of Tournai. [Ghent](/wiki/Ghent "Ghent") had an important local administration and was the location of the Abbey of Saint Bavo, founded by Saint [Amandus](/wiki/Amandus "Amandus"). However, this abbey was suppressed and the canons were removed, moving to the collegiate church of Saint John, and it changed its name to Saint Bavo. This collegiate church became the see of the current diocese. The diocese was created from the surrounding dioceses in Belgium.

## Territorial structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Territorial structure")]

Originally, the diocese was much larger and contained the city of [Hulst](/wiki/Hulst "Hulst"). Currently, the diocese is coextensive with the Belgian province of [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders"), in addition to the [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") of [Zwijndrecht](/wiki/Zwijndrecht,_Belgium "Zwijndrecht, Belgium"), which is in the secular [Province of Antwerp](/wiki/Province_of_Antwerp "Province of Antwerp").

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Administration")]

The diocese produced some important priests and clergy like [Edward Poppe](/wiki/Edward_Poppe "Edward Poppe"). The current bishop is Abbot [Lode Van Hecke](/wiki/Lode_Van_Hecke "Lode Van Hecke") who was appointed by pope Francis in 2019. The diocese is a suffragan of the [Archdiocese of Mechelen-Brussels](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Archdiocese_of_Mechelen%E2%80%93Brussels "Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Mechelen–Brussels").

## Saints

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Saints")]

* [Pharaildis](/wiki/Pharaildis "Pharaildis")
* [Bavo of Ghent](/wiki/Bavo_of_Ghent "Bavo of Ghent")
* [Livinus](/wiki/Livinus "Livinus")

The Bishop of Ghent is the [ordinary](/wiki/Ordinary_(Catholic_Church) "Ordinary (Catholic Church)") of the Diocese of Ghent.

**List of the bishops of the Diocese of Ghent, Belgium**

## Other affiliated bishops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Other affiliated bishops")]

### Coadjutor Bishops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Coadjutor Bishops")]

* [Gustavo Leonardo de Battice](/w/index.php?title=Gustavo_Leonardo_de_Battice&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gustavo Leonardo de Battice (page does not exist)") (1877–1885), did not succeed to see
* [Franciscus Renatus Boussen](/wiki/Franciscus_Renatus_Boussen "Franciscus Renatus Boussen") (1832–1834), did not succeed to see; appointed Bishop of Brugge {Bruges}
* [Honoré-Joseph Coppieters](/w/index.php?title=Honor%C3%A9-Joseph_Coppieters&action=edit&redlink=1 "Honoré-Joseph Coppieters (page does not exist)") (1927)
* [Henri-Charles-Camille Lambrecht](/w/index.php?title=Henri-Charles-Camille_Lambrecht&action=edit&redlink=1 "Henri-Charles-Camille Lambrecht (page does not exist)") (1886–1888)
* [Arthur Luysterman](/wiki/Arthur_Luysterman "Arthur Luysterman") (1990–1991)

### Auxiliary Bishops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Auxiliary Bishops")]

* [Leo-Karel De Kesel](/w/index.php?title=Leo-Karel_De_Kesel&action=edit&redlink=1 "Leo-Karel De Kesel (page does not exist)") (1960–1990)
* [Nicolas French](/w/index.php?title=Nicolas_French&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nicolas French (page does not exist)") (1668–1678)
* [Oscar Jozef Joliet](/w/index.php?title=Oscar_Jozef_Joliet&action=edit&redlink=1 "Oscar Jozef Joliet (page does not exist)") (1948–1969)
* [Eugène van Rechem](/wiki/Eug%C3%A8ne_van_Rechem "Eugène van Rechem") (1914–1943)

### Other priests of this diocese who became bishops

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diocese_of_Ghent&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Other priests of this diocese who became bishops")]

* [Lodewijk Aerts](/wiki/Lodewijk_Aerts "Lodewijk Aerts"), appointed Bishop of Brugge {Bruges} in 2016
* [Josef De Kesel](/wiki/Josef_De_Kesel "Josef De Kesel"), appointed auxiliary bishop of Mechelen-Brussel {Malines-Brussels} in 2002; later cardinal
* [Gustaaf Joos](/wiki/Gustaaf_Joos "Gustaaf Joos"), appointed a titular archbishop and then cardinal in 2003
* [Paul Van den Berghe](/wiki/Paul_Van_den_Berghe "Paul Van den Berghe"), appointed bishop of Antwerp in 1980
* [François Camille Van Ronslé](/w/index.php?title=Fran%C3%A7ois_Camille_Van_Ronsl%C3%A9&action=edit&redlink=1 "François Camille Van Ronslé (page does not exist)") (priest here 1886–1889), appointed Vicar Apostolic of Belgian Congo in 1896
